title: Generate Parentheses
pattern: Backtracking â€” Constrained Choice (Open/Close Count Tracking)
coreIdea: Generate all valid combinations of n pairs of well-formed parentheses by backtracking, adding an open parenthesis whenever the open count is below n, and adding a close parenthesis only when it wouldn't exceed the currently open count.
recognitionCues: asks for all valid combinations of balanced parentheses for a given n
classic backtracking problem with two simple pruning constraints
approach: Recurse with counts of open and close parentheses used so far, and the current string built. Add '(' if open count < n, recurse. Add ')' if close count < open count, recurse. When the string length reaches 2n, add it to results.
timeComplexity: O(4^n / sqrt(n)) â€” Catalan number bound
